sources: DEA List I / List II (narcotics precursors)

danger_levels.critical: Always block. Chemical weapons, nerve agents, CWC Schedule 1.
danger_levels.high: Block by default. Direct weapon/explosive/narcotics precursors.
danger_levels.medium: Warn user. Dual-use, DEA List II, CWC Schedule 3, reportable.
danger_levels.low: Inform user. Monitored substances, low standalone risk.

chemicals.name: 1,10-Bis[N-(3-dimethylcarbamoxy-α-picolyl)-N-ethyl-N-methylammonio]decane-2,9- dione dibromide
chemicals.description: CWC Nerve agents (Carbamates, bisquaternaries of dimethylcarbamoyloxypyridines) — CWC 1A16-2 Example 1 (Family_Example)

chemicals.name:
1-[N,N-dimethyl-N-(2-hydroxy)ethylammonio]-10-[N-(3-dimethylcarbamoxy-
α-picolinyl)-N,N-dimethylammonio]decane dibromide
chemicals.description: CWC Nerve agents (Carbamates, quaternaries of dimethylcarbamoyloxypyridines) — CWC 1A16-1 Example 1 (Family_Example)

chemicals.name: Adamantane
chemicals.description: Precursor to amantadine and related drugs.

chemicals.name: Ammonium nitrate
chemicals.description: Restricted above 16% w/w nitrogen. ANFO explosive component.

chemicals.name: Citric acid derivative context
chemicals.description: List I chemical for heroin preparation.

chemicals.name: Cyanogen bromide
chemicals.description: Extremely toxic. Used as chemical weapon historically.

chemicals.name: D-Glucose / Dextrose (precursor context)
chemicals.description: List I when used for illicit drug dilution.

chemicals.name: Dinitrogen tetroxide
chemicals.description: Powerful oxidizer. Extremely toxic and corrosive gas.

chemicals.name: Hydrogen peroxide
chemicals.description: Restricted above 12% w/w. Used in TATP/HMTD synthesis.

chemicals.name: Isosafrole
chemicals.description: Isomer of safrole, precursor to MDMA.

chemicals.name: Nitric acid
chemicals.description: Restricted above 3% w/w. Used in nitroglycerin/nitrocellulose synthesis.

chemicals.name: Nitromethane
chemicals.description: Restricted above 16% w/w. Explosive fuel component.

chemicals.name: Phenylacetone (P2P)
chemicals.description: Key precursor to amphetamine and methamphetamine.

chemicals.name: Phenylacetonitrile (benzyl cyanide)
chemicals.description: Precursor to phenylacetic acid and amphetamines.

chemicals.name: Phosgene
chemicals.description: Chemical weapon. Lethal choking agent. CWC Schedule 3.

chemicals.name: Piperidine
chemicals.description: Fentanyl family precursor. CWC Schedule 3 / DEA List I.

chemicals.name: Potassium chlorate
chemicals.description: Restricted above 40% w/w. Oxidizer for improvised explosives.

chemicals.name: Potassium perchlorate
chemicals.description: Restricted above 40% w/w. Powerful oxidizer for pyrotechnics/explosives.

chemicals.name: Safrole
chemicals.description: Natural precursor to MDMA. Found in sassafras oil.

chemicals.name: Tryptamine
chemicals.description: Precursor to DMT and psilocybin analogues.

chemicals.name: Acetone
chemicals.description: Reportable. Used in TATP synthesis and narcotics manufacturing.

chemicals.name: Hexachlorobenzene
chemicals.description: Persistent organic pollutant. Banned under Stockholm Convention.

chemicals.name: Hexachloroethane
chemicals.description: Persistent organic pollutant. Toxic smoke agent.

chemicals.name: Isopropanol
chemicals.description: Solvent and reagent in drug manufacturing.

chemicals.name: Sulfuric acid
chemicals.description: Reportable. Used in nitration reactions for explosives.
